scenario: A saas provider runs Camunda 8.8 across 12 microservices that share one Spring Boot job worker codebase. The platform engineers must override settings for one specific worker without changing the global defaults, so they prepare entries under camunda.client.worker.override and need to know which key identifies one entry.
question: Which key identifies a single entry inside the camunda.client.worker.override block?

options.text: The bean name of the worker handler, letting the configuration target one specific Spring component bean
The fully qualified method name of the handler, letting the configuration target one specific worker reference
The job type the worker handles, letting the configuration target one specific Zeebe job-type string
The tenant identifier of the worker, letting the configuration target one specific per-tenant registration

a.text: Incorrect. The bean name is not the override key; the documented key of the override entry is the job type the worker activates.

b.text: Incorrect. The qualified method name is not the override key; the documented key of the override entry is the job type the worker activates.

c.text: Correct. The reference page states that the key of the override is the job type; entries under camunda.client.worker.override.<job-type> overlay the defaults for that specific job type with their own properties.

d.text: Incorrect. The tenant identifier is configured via the tenant-ids array inside the override entry, not used as the override key itself.

explanation: Per-worker overrides in camunda.client.worker.override are keyed by job type. Each override entry sets properties for one specific Zeebe job-type string and replaces or extends the corresponding value declared in camunda.client.worker.defaults.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
